description: In src/lib/satellite/look-angles.ts, observerEci() computes the observer's Earth-centered position using a spherical Earth (a fixed radius ~6378.137 km at every latitude), which is the wrong geodetic model. Fix observerEci to use the WGS84 ellipsoid with flattening, so the observer position magnitude equals the equatorial radius a = 6378.137 km at the equator (lat 0) and the polar radius b = a*(1-f) ≈ 6356.752 km at the pole (lat 90°) — currently the pole is wrong by ~21 km. Use the standard WGS84 constants (a = 6378.137 km, 1/f = 298.257223563). observerEci also takes an altitude argument (km above the ellipsoid); add it along the geodetic normal, i.e. x,y use (N + alt) and z uses (N*(1-e^2) + alt), so a positive altitude increases the position magnitude accordingly.
